title: ferray-stride-tricks: sliding_window_view rejects negative strides — can't window reversed arrays
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

sliding_window_view() explicitly rejects arrays with negative strides: 'sliding_window_view does not support negative strides; make the array contiguous first'. NumPy handles this by requiring the array to be writeable=False (which it is for views). If you reverse an array and try to take a sliding window, ferray errors. The fix should either handle negative strides correctly in the stride computation or auto-contiguify the input.
